notes.note: Ante diem sextum Idus Maias = 10 May 54 BC.
C. Vestorius of Puteoli, banker and shared correspondent of Cicero, Atticus, and the bay-of-Naples circle; a recurring background figure of the 50s and 40s letters.
M. Terentius Varro of Reate, the polymath antiquarian; his books are in Atticus's library, and Cicero is drawing from them for a work in progress (almost certainly De Re Publica, drafted 54-51 BC).
"Festive odorari" --- the verb is the language of hounds running a scent; Atticus has the political nose, Cicero the political mouth.
